title: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

seo.title: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs at home! This easy recipe features tender, marinated short ribs perfect for your next Korean dinner. Get the best flavor!

easy Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs recipe

seo.imageAlt: Platter of grilled Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs with sesame seeds and green onions, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, savory flavor of homemade Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs – perfect for a delicious family dinner or special occasion!

description: Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs are a beloved dish featuring tender, cross-cut short ribs marinated in a sweet and savory soy-garlic sauce. This iconic Korean barbecue staple offers an explosion of flavors, perfect for grilling to caramelized perfection. Enjoy these incredibly juicy and flavorful ribs that are sure to be a crowd-pleaser at any gathering.

instructions.title: Prepare the Short Ribs
instructions.text: Rinse the flanken-cut short ribs under cold water to remove any bone fragments. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. You can lightly score the surface of the ribs to help the marinade penetrate deeper, but this is optional for flanken-cut.

instructions.title: Make the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the soy sauce, brown sugar, grated Asian pear, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, mirin, black pepper, and the 2 chopped green onions. Whisk everything together until the sugar is fully dissolved and the marinade is well combined.

instructions.title: Marinate the Ribs
instructions.text: Place the dried short ribs into the bowl with the marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. If the ribs do not fit flat, you can use a large zip-top bag. Cover the bowl or seal the bag and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight for best flavor.

instructions.title: Grill or Broil the Ribs
instructions.text: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat (about 400-450F / 200-230C) or preheat your broiler. Lightly oil the grill grates. Place the marinated ribs on the hot grill, discarding any excess marinade. Grill for 3-5 minutes per side, or until beautifully caramelized and cooked to your desired doneness. If broiling, place ribs on a foil-lined baking sheet and broil 4-6 inches from the heat for 3-4 minutes per side.

instructions.title: Rest and Slice
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Galbi from the grill or broiler and let them rest on a cutting board for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring tender and juicy ribs. You can slice the meat off the bone into bite-sized pieces if desired, or serve whole.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Arrange the cooked Galbi on a serving platter. Garnish generously with thinly sliced green onions and toasted sesame seeds. Serve immediately with steamed rice, fresh lettuce wraps, and various Korean side dishes (banchan) for a complete and authentic meal.

tips: For maximum tenderness and flavor, marinate the short ribs for at least 12-24 hours. The enzymes in the Asian pear also help to naturally tenderize the meat.

Flanken-cut short ribs are traditional for Galbi as they cook quickly and absorb the marinade well. If you can only find English-cut short ribs, you will need to butterfly them to a thinner, more even thickness for faster cooking.

Galbi is best served fresh off the grill. Pair it with fresh lettuce leaves for wrapping, ssamjang (Korean dipping sauce), kimchi, and other banchan for a complete and authentic Korean BBQ experience.
article:
Galbi, also known as kalbi, is more than just a dish; it's a culinary experience that embodies the heart and soul of Korean barbecue. These succulent, marinated beef short ribs are a staple at Korean dinner tables and restaurants worldwide, celebrated for their tender texture, irresistible sweet and savory glaze, and the joyous communal act of grilling. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Korean BBQ flavor in your own home, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master the art of making Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs.

## What is Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs?

At its core, Galbi refers to grilled ribs, most commonly beef short ribs. The term "galbi" literally means "rib" in Korean. What sets Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs apart is the unique preparation method: beef short ribs are either butterflied (cut along the bone to create a long, thin strip of meat) or cut flanken-style (across the bones, resulting in thin slices with small bone segments). This specific cut maximizes the surface area, allowing the rich marinade to deeply penetrate the meat and tenderize it beautifully.

The marinade is the true star of Galbi, a harmonious blend of soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, sugar or honey, and often a secret ingredient like grated Asian pear or kiwi, which acts as a natural tenderizer. This potent mixture infuses the beef with a complex flavor profile that is both sweet and savory, with a hint of umami. Once marinated, the ribs are traditionally grilled over an open flame, resulting in beautifully caramelized edges and a juicy, tender interior that melts in your mouth. Galbi is considered a premium dish in Korean cuisine, often reserved for special occasions, but increasingly enjoyed as a regular treat due to its universal appeal.

## Ingredients for Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

Creating authentic Galbi requires a few key ingredients, most of which are readily available at Asian markets or even well-stocked supermarkets.

**For the Beef Ribs:**
*   3-4 lbs beef short ribs (flanken-style or butterflied)

**For the Marinade:**
*   1 cup soy sauce (low sodium preferred)
*   1/2 cup brown sugar or honey
*   1/4 cup mirin (rice wine for cooking)
*   1/4 cup sesame oil
*   1/4 cup water or beef broth
*   1 whole Asian pear, grated (or 1/2 kiwi, grated, or 1/4 yellow onion, grated)
*   6-8 cloves garlic, minced
*   1-inch piece fresh ginger, grated
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   2-3 green onions, thinly sliced (for marinade and garnish)

**Optional Garnishes:**
*   Toasted sesame seeds
*   Extra sliced green onions
*   Red chili threads

## How to Make Galbi: Step-by-Step Instructions

Making delicious Galbi at home is simpler than you might think, though it requires a bit of foresight for marinating.

**Step 1: Prepare the Ribs**
If using flanken-style ribs, rinse them under cold water to remove any bone fragments. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. If using butterflied ribs, ensure they are uniform in thickness. Some prefer to score the meat lightly in a crosshatch pattern to help the marinade penetrate deeper and prevent curling during grilling. Trim any excessive fat, but leave some for flavor and moisture.

**Step 2: Make the Marinade**
In a large mixing bowl, combine the soy sauce, brown sugar (or honey), mirin, sesame oil, water (or beef broth), grated Asian pear (or kiwi/onion), minced garlic, grated ginger, and black pepper. Whisk everything together until the sugar is fully dissolved. Stir in about half of the sliced green onions.

**Step 3: Marinate the Ribs**
Place the prepared short ribs into the marinade. Ensure all pieces are fully submerged and coated. You can use a large bowl, a baking dish, or a sturdy zip-top bag. Cover the bowl/dish or seal the bag and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, but ideally overnight (12-24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderness. The longer the marinade time, the more flavorful and tender your Galbi will be.

**Step 4: Grill the Galbi**
When ready to cook, remove the ribs from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes before grilling to bring them closer to room temperature. Preheat your grill (charcoal, gas, or an indoor grill pan) to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.
Place the marinated ribs on the hot grill. Grill for 3-5 minutes per side, depending on the thickness of the ribs and your desired doneness. You're looking for beautiful caramelization and slight charring on the edges. Be careful not to overcook, as short ribs can dry out quickly. Flip them frequently to ensure even cooking and prevent burning due to the sugar in the marinade.

**Step 5: Rest and Serve**
Once cooked, transfer the Galbi to a cutting board or platter. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in more tender and flavorful meat. After resting, slice the meat off the bones (if flanken style) into bite-sized pieces, or simply serve the butterflied ribs whole. Garnish with the remaining fresh green onions and toasted sesame seeds.

## Tips for Perfect Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs

Achieving restaurant-quality Galbi at home is entirely possible with a few expert tips:

*   **Quality Meat is Key:** Start with well-marbled beef short ribs. The fat content contributes significantly to the flavor and juiciness.
*   **Don't Skimp on Marinating Time:** Seriously, an overnight marinade makes all the difference for flavor absorption and tenderness.
*   **The Tenderizer Secret:** Asian pear, kiwi, or onion in the marinade contains enzymes that naturally break down meat fibers. Kiwi is particularly potent, so use it sparingly (1/2 a small kiwi for 3-4 lbs of meat) and don't marinate for more than 6-8 hours with kiwi, or your meat can become mushy.
*   **High Heat, Quick Cook:** Galbi cooks best over high heat for a relatively short period. This creates that desirable char and keeps the inside juicy. Don't overcrowd the grill, as it will lower the temperature and steam the meat instead of searing it.
*   **Watch for Burning:** The sugar in the marinade can cause quick charring. Keep an eye on the ribs and flip them as needed to prevent burning.
*   **Rest Your Meat:** This simple step is crucial for juicy, tender results. Don't skip it!

## Serving Suggestions for Galbi

Galbi is meant to be enjoyed as part of a larger Korean meal experience, often referred to as "ssam" (wraps).

*   **Lettuce Wraps (Ssam):** Serve your Galbi with an assortment of fresh lettuce leaves (red leaf, green leaf, perilla leaves) for wrapping.
*   **Ssamjang:** This savory, spicy dipping sauce made from doenjang (fermented soybean paste) and gochujang (chili paste) is essential for ssam.
*   **Banchan (Side Dishes):** A variety of small Korean side dishes elevates the meal. Kimchi (fermented cabbage), pickled radishes, seasoned bean sprouts, and cucumber salad are popular choices.
*   **Steamed Rice:** A bowl of warm, fluffy white rice is the perfect accompaniment to soak up the delicious juices.
*   **Garlic and Chilis:** Raw garlic slices and green chili peppers are often added to lettuce wraps for an extra kick.
*   **Soup:** A light soup like Doenjang Jjigae (soybean paste stew) or Kimchi Jjigae (kimchi stew) can round out the meal beautifully.

## Variations and Substitutions

While Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs are a classic, there are a few ways to adapt the recipe to your preferences or available ingredients:

*   **Pork Galbi (Dwaeji Galbi):** If beef short ribs are hard to find or you prefer pork, use pork spare ribs or pork shoulder sliced thin. The marinade can remain largely the same, though you might adjust sweetness slightly.
*   **Spicy Galbi:** For those who love heat, add a tablespoon or two of gochujang (Korean chili paste) to the marinade.
*   **Different Cuts:** While short ribs are traditional, you can adapt the marinade for other cuts of beef like thinly sliced ribeye or even chicken thighs, though cooking times will vary significantly.
*   **Indoor Cooking:** No outdoor grill? No problem! Use a cast-iron grill pan on your stovetop for similar charring. You can also bake the ribs in the oven at 400°F (200°C) for 15-20 minutes, then broil for a few minutes to get some caramelization.

Making Galbi Korean BBQ Short Ribs at home is a rewarding culinary adventure that brings the vibrant flavors of Korea right to your dinner table. With its tender meat, complex marinade, and satisfying grilling experience, Galbi is more than just a meal—it's an invitation to gather, share, and savor truly delicious food. Don't hesitate to try this iconic Korean dinner recipe; your taste buds will thank you!
